summary: Covert console hijack exfiltrates host logs to attacker-controlled Telegram/Firebase
details: On import, index.js replaces the global console with a Proxy that routes every console.* call to a hardcoded Telegram bot (token 1998266306:AAFl_..., chat IDs including -1001482347974) and, for console.dir, PUTs payloads to iiilll.firebaseio.com, a static pattern match). Hardcoded third-party credentials (imgbb key, Telegram bot token) are shipped in the module, and the exfiltration is concealed via Proxy-based behavior hiding. Package name '0e' has no legitimate described purpose. This combines covert exfiltration to attacker-controlled infrastructure, credential redistribution, and obfuscation — three independent block signals.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
